text: Handling deadlines is an essential skill in both academic and professional life. Good time management helps you break large projects into smaller tasks, making them less overwhelming. Prioritization is equally important—focus on urgent or high-impact tasks first, and leave less critical work for later. Scope management means knowing when to say no or adjust expectations, so you don’t overcommit. Finally, communication is key: if you see a deadline becoming impossible, inform your manager or team early. This allows others to help or adjust the timeline. By combining these strategies, you can meet deadlines with less stress and more confidence.
